1	# Persona Lifecycle — Library vs Project Policy + UI Scope
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-22
4	Bead: `ddx-9e0eff22`
5	
6	## Implementation map
7	
8	| Area | Files |
9	|------|-------|
10	| Loader + source tracking | `cli/internal/persona/loader.go`, `cli/internal/persona/types.go` |
11	| Project-local CRUD | `cli/internal/persona/writer.go` |
12	| CLI subcommands | `cli/cmd/persona.go`, `cli/cmd/command_factory_commands.go` |
13	| GraphQL schema + resolvers | `cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_meta.go` |
14	| UI | `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/personas/{data.ts,PersonasView.svelte}` |
15	| Tests | `cli/internal/persona/project_personas_test.go`, `cli/cmd/persona_lifecycle_test.go`, `cli/internal/server/graphql/persona_lifecycle_test.go`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/personas/personas.e2e.ts` |
16	
17	## Problem
18	
19	The personas page is read-only with no explanation of why. A reader cannot tell
20	whether the feature is unfinished or intentional, and teams who want a
21	project-flavored persona ("our code-reviewer, with our opinions") have no
22	sanctioned path: they can fork the library file and push it upstream
23	(inappropriate), never customize (bad outcomes), or scatter ad-hoc files.
24	
25	## D1 — Policy: library vs project content
26	
27	Personas can live in two places:
28	
29	- **Library personas** — `<library>/personas/*.md`. Shipped with DDx, shared
30	  across projects, pulled via `ddx update`, contributed upstream via
31	  `ddx contribute`. **Read-only in UI and API.** Edits are refused with a
32	  typed error; the only mutation affecting library content is `fork`, which
33	  copies the file into the project.
34	
35	- **Project-local personas** — `.ddx/personas/*.md` under the project root.
36	  Live with the project in its VCS. Fully editable via UI, GraphQL, and CLI.
37	  Never pushed upstream by `ddx contribute`.
38	
39	### Override rule
40	
41	When a project-local persona has the same name as a library persona, the
42	project-local file wins for that project's bindings and list views. List
43	views expose one effective persona per name; the source badge becomes
44	`project` for an override so the reader can see that project-local content
45	is active. Load order: project first, then library; duplicate names collapse
46	to the project-local file.
47	
48	### What this does not decide
49	
50	- Multi-project / org-level persona sharing — out of scope.
51	- Rich in-browser markdown editor — a plain `<textarea>` is enough for v1.
52	- Upstreaming project personas — still happens via `ddx contribute`.
53	- Changing how personas get injected into agent prompts — server read path
54	  is unchanged.
55	
56	## D2 — UI scope
57	
58	Minimum viable additions to
59	`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas`:
60	
61	1. **Explainer copy** in the page header. Two sentences: what personas are,
62	   and how library vs project sources differ.
63	2. **Source badge** on each row: `library` or `project`.
64	3. **New persona** button opens a form (name, roles, description, tags,
65	   body). Creates `.ddx/personas/<name>.md` with YAML frontmatter.
66	4. **Edit** affordance on project-source rows only.
67	5. **Delete** affordance on project-source rows only, with confirm.
68	6. **Fork to project** action on library-source rows. Copies the library
69	   file to `.ddx/personas/<name>.md`; if a collision exists, the form
70	   defaults to `<name>-local`.
71	7. **Empty-project hint** when no project personas exist: "No project
72	   personas yet. Fork a library persona or create a new one."
73	8. **Existing bind flow is untouched.**
74	
75	## CLI parity (D3)
76	
77	Same operations surfaced through:
78	
79	- `ddx persona new <name>` — scaffold `.ddx/personas/<name>.md`.
80	- `ddx persona edit <name>` — open `$EDITOR` on the project-local file;
81	  errors on library personas.
82	- `ddx persona fork <library-name> [--as <new-name>]` — copy library into
83	  project.
84	- `ddx persona delete <name>` — project-only; errors on library.
85	
86	UI and CLI share the same `cli/internal/persona` operations:
87	`CreateProjectPersona`, `UpdateProjectPersona`, `DeleteProjectPersona`,
88	`ForkPersonaToProject`. No duplicated write logic.
89	
90	## Error model
91	
92	All project-mutating operations check source first:
93	
94	- If the target persona's source is `library`, return
95	  `PersonaError{Type: ErrorReadOnlyLibrary}` with message
96	  `"persona <name> is a library persona and cannot be <op>; fork it first"`.
97	- If the project file already exists on create, return
98	  `PersonaError{Type: ErrorAlreadyExists}`.
99	- If the project file does not exist on update/delete, return
100	  `PersonaError{Type: ErrorPersonaNotFound}`.
101	
102	GraphQL mutations surface these as typed errors so the UI can show a
103	targeted message.
104

3	// Persona represents an AI personality definition
4	type Persona struct {
5		// Name is the unique identifier for the persona
6		Name string `yaml:"name"`
7	
8		// Roles is the list of roles this persona can fulfill
9		Roles []string `yaml:"roles"`
10	
11		// Description is a brief description of the persona's approach
12		Description string `yaml:"description"`
13	
14		// Tags are keywords for discovery and categorization
15		Tags []string `yaml:"tags"`
16	
17		// Content is the markdown content (body) of the persona
18		Content string `yaml:"-"` // Not included in YAML frontmatter
19	
20		// Source identifies where the persona came from: "library" or "project".
21		// Library personas are read-only; project personas are editable.
22		Source string `yaml:"-"`
23	
24		// FilePath is the absolute path on disk where the persona lives.
25		FilePath string `yaml:"-"`
26	}
27	
28	// Source constants identify where a persona came from.
29	const (
30		SourceLibrary = "library"
31		SourceProject = "project"
32	)

99	// PersonaError represents persona-specific errors
100	type PersonaError struct {
101		Type    string
102		Message string
103		Cause   error
104	}
105	
106	func (e *PersonaError) Error() string {
107		if e.Cause != nil {
108			return e.Message + ": " + e.Cause.Error()
109		}
110		return e.Message
111	}
112	
113	func (e *PersonaError) Unwrap() error {
114		return e.Cause
115	}

3	import (

12	// TestLoader_ProjectOverridesLibrary covers the precedence rule in AC #2:
13	// when a project persona shares a name with a library persona, the project
14	// persona wins for list/load/find operations.
15	func TestLoader_ProjectOverridesLibrary(t *testing.T) {
16		t.Parallel()
17	
18		tmp := t.TempDir()
19		libDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "library")
20		projDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "project")
21		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0o755))
22		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projDir, 0o755))
23	
24		libBody := `---
25	name: code-reviewer
26	roles: [code-reviewer]
27	description: Library reviewer
28	tags: []
29	---
30	
31	# Library Reviewer
32	`
33		projBody := `---
34	name: code-reviewer
35	roles: [code-reviewer]
36	description: Project reviewer
37	tags: []
38	---
39	
40	# Project Reviewer
41	`
42		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libDir, "code-reviewer.md"), []byte(libBody), 0o644))
43		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projDir, "code-reviewer.md"), []byte(projBody), 0o644))
44	
45		loader := NewPersonaLoaderWithDirs(libDir, projDir)
46	
47		loaded, err := loader.LoadPersona("code-reviewer")
48		require.NoError(t, err)
49		assert.Equal(t, SourceProject, loaded.Source, "project persona should win on name collision")
50		assert.Equal(t, "Project reviewer", loaded.Description)
51	
52		all, err := loader.ListPersonas()
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		require.Len(t, all, 1, "duplicate names collapse; project wins")
55		assert.Equal(t, SourceProject, all[0].Source)
56	}
57	
58	// TestLoader_SourceBadges covers AC #2: list distinguishes library vs
59	// project personas with correct source labels.
60	func TestLoader_SourceBadges(t *testing.T) {
61		t.Parallel()
62	
63		tmp := t.TempDir()
64		libDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "library")
65		projDir := filepath.Join(tmp, "project")
66		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0o755))
67		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projDir, 0o755))
68	
69		writeFixture(t, libDir, "architect", "Library architect")
70		writeFixture(t, projDir, "our-reviewer", "Project reviewer")
71	
72		loader := NewPersonaLoaderWithDirs(libDir, projDir)
73		all, err := loader.ListPersonas()
74		require.NoError(t, err)
75	
76		got := map[string]string{}
77		for _, p := range all {
78			got[p.Name] = p.Source
79		}
80		assert.Equal(t, SourceLibrary, got["architect"])
81		assert.Equal(t, SourceProject, got["our-reviewer"])
82	}
83	
84	// TestProjectPersonaWriter covers AC #2/#3: project-local CRUD succeeds,
85	// library personas are read-only, and fork produces a project file.
86	func TestProjectPersonaWriter(t *testing.T) {
87		t.Parallel()
88	
89		workingDir := t.TempDir()
90		libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(workingDir, "lib", "personas")
91		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))
92		writeFixture(t, libPersonasDir, "architect", "Library architect")
93	
94		// Point resolveLibraryPersonasDir at libDir by synthesizing a config
95		// file that the loader will pick up.
96		configPath := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "config.yaml")
97		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(configPath), 0o755))
98		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(
99			"version: \"2.0\"\nlibrary:\n  path: lib\n"), 0o644))
100	
101		writer := NewProjectPersonaWriter(workingDir)
102	
103		// Create a project-local persona.
104		body := `---
105	name: ignored
106	roles: [code-reviewer]
107	description: Our reviewer
108	tags: []
109	---
110	
111	# Our Reviewer
112	`
113		created, err := writer.Create("our-reviewer", body)
114		require.NoError(t, err)
115		assert.Equal(t, SourceProject, created.Source)
116		assert.Equal(t, "our-reviewer", created.Name, "name is forced to match filename")
117	
118		// Library personas cannot be updated or deleted directly.
119		_, err = writer.Update("architect", body)
120		require.Error(t, err)
121		perr, ok := err.(*PersonaError)
122		require.True(t, ok)
123		assert.Equal(t, ErrorReadOnlyLibrary, perr.Type)
124	
125		err = writer.Delete("architect")
126		require.Error(t, err)
127		perr, ok = err.(*PersonaError)
128		require.True(t, ok)
129		assert.Equal(t, ErrorReadOnlyLibrary, perr.Type)
130	
131		// Fork copies the library persona into the project dir.
132		forked, err := writer.Fork("architect", "architect-local")
133		require.NoError(t, err)
134		assert.Equal(t, SourceProject, forked.Source)
135		assert.Equal(t, "architect-local", forked.Name)
136	
137		// Update then delete the project persona succeeds.
138		_, err = writer.Update("our-reviewer", body)
139		require.NoError(t, err)
140		require.NoError(t, writer.Delete("our-reviewer"))
141		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "personas", "our-reviewer.md"))
142		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
143	}
144	
145	func writeFixture(t *testing.T, dir, name, description string) {
146		t.Helper()
147		body := "---\nname: " + name + "\nroles: [code-reviewer]\ndescription: " + description + "\ntags: []\n---\n\n# " + name + "\n"
148		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, name+".md"), []byte(body), 0o644))
149	}

14	// TestPersonaCLI_Lifecycle verifies the new CLI subcommands — new, edit,
15	// fork, delete — against AC #4 (CLI parity): project-local files get
16	// created/updated/deleted, and library personas are protected.
17	func TestPersonaCLI_Lifecycle(t *testing.T) {
18		workDir := t.TempDir()
19		libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
20		libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
21		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))
22	
23		libPersona := `---
24	name: architect
25	roles: [architect]
26	description: Library architect
27	tags: []
28	---
29	
30	# Architect
31	`
32		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPersonasDir, "architect.md"), []byte(libPersona), 0o644))
33	
34		cfg := "version: \"2.0\"\nlibrary:\n  path: library\n"
35		ddxDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx")
36		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
37		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))
38	
39		bodyFile := filepath.Join(workDir, "body.md")
40		bodyContent := `---
41	name: ignored
42	roles: [code-reviewer]
43	description: Our reviewer
44	tags: []
45	---
46	
47	# Our Reviewer
48	`
49		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bodyFile, []byte(bodyContent), 0o644))
50	
51		run := func(args ...string) (string, error) {
52			rootCmd := getPersonaIntegrationTestRootCommand(workDir)
53			var out bytes.Buffer
54			rootCmd.SetOut(&out)
55			rootCmd.SetErr(&out)
56			rootCmd.SetArgs(args)
57			err := rootCmd.Execute()
58			return out.String(), err
59		}
60	
61		// new: creates a project-local persona.
62		out, err := run("persona", "new", "our-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile)
63		require.NoError(t, err, out)
64		projectFile := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "our-reviewer.md")
65		assert.FileExists(t, projectFile)
66	
67		// edit: updates via --body.
68		out, err = run("persona", "edit", "our-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile)
69		require.NoError(t, err, out)
70	
71		// fork: copies the library persona into the project dir.
72		out, err = run("persona", "fork", "architect", "--as", "architect-local")
73		require.NoError(t, err, out)
74		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "architect-local.md"))
75	
76		// edit: library persona is rejected.
77		_, err = run("persona", "edit", "architect", "--body", bodyFile)
78		require.Error(t, err)
79		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "library")
80	
81		// delete: library persona is rejected.
82		_, err = run("persona", "delete", "architect")
83		require.Error(t, err)
84		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "library")
85	
86		// delete: project-local succeeds.
87		out, err = run("persona", "delete", "our-reviewer")
88		require.NoError(t, err, out)
89		_, err = os.Stat(projectFile)
90		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
91	}

93	// TestPersonaCLI_GraphQLParity covers AC #4: a persona created via `ddx
94	// persona new` is visible to the persona package's loader, proving that
95	// the CLI and GraphQL paths share the same storage.
96	func TestPersonaCLI_GraphQLParity(t *testing.T) {
97		workDir := t.TempDir()
98		libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
99		libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
100		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755))
101		libPersona := "---\nname: architect\nroles: [architect]\ndescription: Library architect\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Architect\n"
102		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPersonasDir, "architect.md"), []byte(libPersona), 0o644))
103	
104		cfg := "version: \"2.0\"\nlibrary:\n  path: library\n"
105		ddxDir := filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx")
106		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
107		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644))
108	
109		bodyFile := filepath.Join(workDir, "body.md")
110		body := "---\nname: ignored\nroles: [code-reviewer]\ndescription: Shared reviewer\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Shared\n"
111		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(bodyFile, []byte(body), 0o644))
112	
113		rootCmd := getPersonaIntegrationTestRootCommand(workDir)
114		var buf bytes.Buffer
115		rootCmd.SetOut(&buf)
116		rootCmd.SetErr(&buf)
117		rootCmd.SetArgs([]string{"persona", "new", "shared-reviewer", "--body", bodyFile})
118		require.NoError(t, rootCmd.Execute(), buf.String())
119	
120		loader := persona.NewPersonaLoader(workDir)
121		all, err := loader.ListPersonas()
122		require.NoError(t, err)
123	
124		names := map[string]string{}
125		for _, p := range all {
126			names[p.Name] = p.Source
127		}
128		assert.Equal(t, persona.SourceProject, names["shared-reviewer"],
129			"CLI-created persona should show up via the persona loader as a project persona")
130		assert.Equal(t, persona.SourceLibrary, names["architect"],
131			"library architect still visible with library source")
132	}

16	// TestIntegration_PersonaLifecycle covers AC #3 and AC #4:
17	//   - personaCreate/personaUpdate/personaDelete persist to `.ddx/personas`.
18	//   - Mutations gated on source: library personas are rejected with a typed error.
19	//   - A CLI-initiated create is then visible to the GraphQL `personas` query,
20	//     proving that UI and CLI share the same storage.
21	func TestIntegration_PersonaLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
22		workDir, store := setupIntegrationDir(t)
23		_ = store
24	
25		// Seed a library persona under the test workDir. The default library
26		// path resolution points at `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas`, but to keep
27		// the test hermetic we override DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH.
28		libraryRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, "library")
29		libPersonasDir := filepath.Join(libraryRoot, "personas")
30		if err := os.MkdirAll(libPersonasDir, 0o755); err != nil {
31			t.Fatal(err)
32		}
33		libBody := `---
34	name: architect
35	roles: [architect]
36	description: Library architect
37	tags: []
38	---
39	
40	# Library Architect
41	`
42		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPersonasDir, "architect.md"), []byte(libBody), 0o644); err != nil {
43			t.Fatal(err)
44		}
45	
46		// Rewrite the ddx config to point at libraryRoot.
47		cfg := `version: "2.0"
48	library:
49	  path: ` + libraryRoot + `
50	bead:
51	  id_prefix: "it"
52	`
53		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "config.yaml"), []byte(cfg), 0o644); err != nil {
54			t.Fatal(err)
55		}
56		t.Setenv("DDX_LIBRARY_BASE_PATH", libraryRoot)
57	
58		state := newTestStateProvider(workDir, store)
59		h := newGQLHandler(state, workDir, nil)
60		projectID := state.projects[0].ID
61	
62		// AC#3: create a new project persona.
63		newBody := `---\nname: our-reviewer\nroles: [code-reviewer]\ndescription: Our reviewer\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Our Reviewer\n`
64		// JSON-escape the body.
65		createQuery := `mutation($name: String!, $body: String!, $pid: String!) {
66			personaCreate(name: $name, body: $body, projectId: $pid) { id name source }
67		}`
68		resp := gqlMutation(t, h, createQuery, map[string]any{
69			"name": "our-reviewer",
70			"body": newBody,
71			"pid":  projectID,
72		})
73		if resp["data"] == nil {
74			t.Fatalf("no data: %s", resp["errors"])
75		}
76		assertFileExists(t, filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "our-reviewer.md"))
77	
78		// AC#3: update the project persona.
79		updateQuery := `mutation($name: String!, $body: String!, $pid: String!) {
80			personaUpdate(name: $name, body: $body, projectId: $pid) { id name source }
81		}`
82		updatedBody := `---\nname: our-reviewer\nroles: [code-reviewer]\ndescription: Our updated reviewer\ntags: []\n---\n\n# Our Reviewer (updated)\n`
83		_ = gqlMutation(t, h, updateQuery, map[string]any{
84			"name": "our-reviewer",
85			"body": updatedBody,
86			"pid":  projectID,
87		})
88	
89		// AC#3: library persona cannot be updated.
90		resp = gqlMutation(t, h, updateQuery, map[string]any{
91			"name": "architect",
92			"body": updatedBody,
93			"pid":  projectID,
94		})
95		if !bytes.Contains([]byte(resp["errors"]), []byte("persona_is_library_read_only")) {
96			t.Fatalf("expected typed library error, got: %s", resp["errors"])
97		}
98	
99		// AC#3: library persona cannot be deleted.
100		deleteQuery := `mutation($name: String!, $pid: String!) {
101			personaDelete(name: $name, projectId: $pid) { ok name }
102		}`
103		resp = gqlMutation(t, h, deleteQuery, map[string]any{
104			"name": "architect",
105			"pid":  projectID,
106		})
107		if !bytes.Contains([]byte(resp["errors"]), []byte("persona_is_library_read_only")) {
108			t.Fatalf("expected typed library error, got: %s", resp["errors"])
109		}
110	
111		// AC#4: CLI parity — create a persona using the writer directly, then
112		// verify GraphQL personas query sees it.
113		cliBodyPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "cli-persona.md")
114		cliBody := "---\nname: ignored\nroles: [implementer]\ndescription: Made via CLI\ntags: []\n---\n\n# CLI\n"
115		if err := os.WriteFile(cliBodyPath, []byte(cliBody), 0o644); err != nil {
116			t.Fatal(err)
117		}
118		runPersonaCommand(t, workDir, "persona", "new", "cli-made", "--body", cliBodyPath)
119	
120		listQuery := `query($pid: String) { personas(projectId: $pid) { name source } }`
121		resp = gqlMutation(t, h, listQuery, map[string]any{"pid": projectID})
122		if !bytes.Contains([]byte(resp["data"]), []byte("cli-made")) {
123			t.Fatalf("expected cli-made in personas list, got: %s", resp["data"])
124		}
125		if !bytes.Contains([]byte(resp["data"]), []byte("our-reviewer")) {
126			t.Fatalf("expected our-reviewer in personas list, got: %s", resp["data"])
127		}
128		// AC#2: architect persona visible with library source.
129		if !bytes.Contains([]byte(resp["data"]), []byte(`"name":"architect","source":"library"`)) {
130			t.Fatalf("expected architect with library source, got: %s", resp["data"])
131		}
132	
133		// AC#3: project delete succeeds.
134		resp = gqlMutation(t, h, deleteQuery, map[string]any{
135			"name": "our-reviewer",
136			"pid":  projectID,
137		})
138		if resp["errors"] != nil {
139			t.Fatalf("unexpected errors: %s", resp["errors"])
140		}
141		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "our-reviewer.md")); !os.IsNotExist(err) {
142			t.Fatalf("expected our-reviewer.md removed; got err=%v", err)
143		}
144	
145		// AC#3: fork library into project.
146		forkQuery := `mutation($ln: String!, $new: String, $pid: String!) {
147			personaFork(libraryName: $ln, newName: $new, projectId: $pid) { name source }
148		}`
149		newName := "architect-local"
150		resp = gqlMutation(t, h, forkQuery, map[string]any{
151			"ln":  "architect",
152			"new": newName,
153			"pid": projectID,
154		})
155		if resp["errors"] != nil {
156			t.Fatalf("fork error: %s", resp["errors"])
157		}
158		assertFileExists(t, filepath.Join(workDir, ".ddx", "personas", "architect-local.md"))
159	}

161	func runPersonaCommand(t *testing.T, workDir string, args ...string) {
162		t.Helper()
163		root := ddxcmd.NewCommandFactory(workDir).NewRootCommand()
164		var out bytes.Buffer
165		root.SetOut(&out)
166		root.SetErr(&out)
167		root.SetArgs(args)
168		if err := root.Execute(); err != nil {
169			t.Fatalf("ddx %s failed: %v\n%s", strings.Join(args, " "), err, out.String())
170		}
171	}

174		t.Helper()

176			t.Fatalf("expected %s to exist: %v", path, err)
177		}

180	// gqlMutation sends a GraphQL request with variables and returns the raw
181	// parsed body, letting the caller inspect either data or errors.

185		// JSON-marshaling.
186		for k, v := range vars {
187			if s, ok := v.(string); ok {
188				vars[k] = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\\n", "\n")
189			}
190		}
191		payload := map[string]any{
192			"query":     query,
193			"variables": vars,
194		}
195		body, _ := json.Marshal(payload)
196		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/graphql", bytes.NewReader(body))
197		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
198		w := httptest.NewRecorder()
199		h.ServeHTTP(w, req)
200		var resp map[string]json.RawMessage
